mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% and HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST declines, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% signals expanding supply-side capacity and productivity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a favorable Phillips curve regime where wage growth does not trigger inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 0.2% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed is in a restrictive tightening regime that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint restrictive regime does not produce lower returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million annualized units signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking economic optimism, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where HOUST > 1.2 million, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary pressure where supply constraints meet weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction between supply contraction and labor weakness is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
